subject: LU-3160 clio: don't ignore layout on writeback

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Patch Set 1:

Niu, could you please add the following test parameters into the commit message so as to verify the patch?

 Test-Parameters: envdefinitions=DURATION=7200 \
 clientdistro=el6 serverdistro=el6 clientcount=4 \
 osscount=2 mdscount=2 austeroptions=-R failover=true \
 useiscsi=true testlist=recovery-random-scale
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 7:

About the failure of recovery random scale: under the help of Chris, I got the log and took a look at it.

Everything seems fine. When maloo thought timeout happened in 3789 seconds, client already wrote ~13G data and it's still writing. Considering the nodes are virtual machines and there are many MDT failure, I would like to make a conclusion that there is nothing wrong with the patch. We should have used a shorter file length under kvm env.

the recovery-scale-random failure is due to too short of a timeout, judging by the pass history, most of succesful runs took much longer

parents.subject: LU-2139 osc: Track and limit "unstable" pages

commit.subject: LU-3160 clio: don't ignore layout on writeback
commit.message:
LU-3160 clio: don't ignore layout on writeback

In some cases such as kernel writeback, we shouldn't ignore the
layout, otherwise, it could race with layout change undergoing.

Test-Parameters: envdefinitions=DURATION=7200  clientdistro=el6 serverdistro=el6 clientcount=4  osscount=2 mdscount=2 austeroptions=-R failover=true  useiscsi=true testlist=recovery-random-scale
Signed-off-by: Niu Yawei <yawei.niu@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ib9d0aa581de90711c92db4c631c52f1950ad5b67
